panels.title: Action / Adventure — things to investigate
panels.content:
## Game state and area

Action-adventure games change their active area or room constantly, and the room ID is one of the most frequently useful values in the genre. A state index that covers gameplay, menus, cutscenes, and game over screens is also worth finding early — many other searches scope to specific states.

## Bitmasks and flag arrays

Boss defeat flags, key item acquisition, ability unlocks, and door states are almost always packed into bitmasks. A single byte can track eight separate yes/no states. Watching for values that change by a power of 2 rather than by 1 is the signal to investigate individual bits.

## Player position

Player X and Y coordinates are worth investigating early in games with hidden rooms or Easter eggs. Once the coordinate system is understood, achievements can be gated on the player entering a specific area.

## Timers

Play timers or stage timers may be a float, frame counter, integer in hex, BCD, centiseconds in a single address, or split across hours, minutes, seconds, and centiseconds. Search for a changing value during active gameplay and investigate each format.

## Death counter

Many action-adventure games silently track total deaths. This value is often stored near other save-persistent data and can support fun achievements that most players won't know to expect.

## Clears per character

If the game has multiple playable characters, each may have independent completion flags within the save data structure.

panels.title: Protection
panels.infobox: Identify these before writing achievement logic.

entry_Protection.name: Demo mode
entry_Protection.tNote: Check for a specific value that is set or cleared only on the main menu or when demo mode initiates. May be a standalone flag or part of a larger game state index.

entry_Protection.name: Save protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Find values that match a loading state or last loaded in-game state so that you can confirm the player is in-game for at least one frame by checking Delta = in-game.

entry_Protection.name: Cheat protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Investigate old guides or walkthroughs for possible cheats. If they offer an advantage, block them. Look for on/off flags, unnatural resource increases during a menu or pause scenario.

entry_Protection.name: Invincibility cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, damageless achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Infinite health cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, HP-based challenge achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: All items / abilities cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, collection achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: All areas unlocked cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, progression achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: No-clip / walk through walls
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, it may allow skipping required areas. Investigate whether achievements need to block it.

entry_Protection.name: New Game+ flag
entry_Protection.tNote: Whether the current run is NG+. Affects item and enemy availability. Investigate whether this needs to be allowed or blocked per achievement.

panels.title: Progression
panels.infobox: Values that track forward movement through areas, story beats, and boss encounters.

entry_Progression.name: Room / area ID
entry_Progression.tNote: The identifier for the current room or area. Changes on every transition.

entry_Progression.name: Game state / screen index
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes between active gameplay, menus, cutscenes, and game over.

entry_Progression.name: Difficulty setting
entry_Progression.tNote: The selected difficulty. May be encoded into save data rather than active RAM.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 1
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether boss 1 has been permanently defeated. May be an individual flag or a bit in a shared byte.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 2
entry_Progression.tNote: Same investigation as boss 1.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 3
entry_Progression.tNote: Continue for each mandatory boss.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 4
entry_Progression.tNote: Continue for each mandatory boss.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flags (combined)
entry_Progression.tNote: All boss states may be packed into a single byte rather than at individual addresses.

entry_Progression.name: Key item acquisition flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Which story-critical items have been obtained. Frequently a bitmask.

entry_Progression.name: Story / event flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Flags for story beats, NPC events, and cutscenes.

entry_Progression.name: Door / area unlock state
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether specific locked areas or gates have been opened.

entry_Progression.name: Ability unlock flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Which movement or combat abilities have been acquired.

entry_Progression.name: Map reveal flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Which areas of the map have been visited.

entry_Progression.name: Quest stage / objective index
entry_Progression.tNote: The current step of the active quest or objective sequence.

entry_Progression.name: Party member recruited
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether additional characters have joined, if the game has a party system.

entry_Progression.name: Game completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value set when the credits roll or the final screen is reached.

entry_Progression.name: Ending variant
entry_Progression.tNote: Which ending was reached, for games with multiple outcomes.

entry_Progression.name: Clears per character
entry_Progression.tNote: If the game has multiple playable characters, where per-character completion is stored.

entry_Progression.name: NG+ / second loop flag
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether the player is on a second or subsequent loop.

entry_Progression.name: Total area transitions
entry_Progression.tNote: A count of room transitions, if one exists.

panels.title: Collections
panels.infobox: Things the player owns or has unlocked that persist across sessions.

entry_Collection.name: Currency / resource count
entry_Collection.tNote: The main spendable resource — rupees, coins, souls, or equivalent.

entry_Collection.name: Equipment — shield
entry_Collection.tNote: Currently equipped shield, if the game has one.

entry_Collection.name: Equipment — accessory
entry_Collection.tNote: Currently equipped accessory or secondary item.

entry_Collection.name: Collectible flags — area 0
entry_Collection.tNote: Which collectibles in area 0 have been permanently gathered. May be a bitmask.

entry_Collection.name: Collectible flags — area 1
entry_Collection.tNote: Area 1. Compare address offset from area 0 to determine entry size.

entry_Collection.name: Collectible flags — area 2
entry_Collection.tNote: Area 2. The same offset pattern from area 1 should apply.

entry_Collection.name: Collectible total (count)
entry_Collection.tNote: A global count of all collectibles gathered, if one exists.

entry_Collection.name: Ability unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: All unlocked abilities or skills tracked together.

entry_Collection.name: Character unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which alternate playable characters have been unlocked.

entry_Collection.name: Chest / container opened flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which treasure chests or containers have been permanently opened.

entry_Collection.name: Side quest completion flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which optional quests have been completed.

panels.title: Challenges
panels.infobox: Values for achievements with pass/fail conditions.

entry_Challenge.name: Player HP (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current health.

entry_Challenge.name: Player HP (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Maximum health. Useful for ratio-based and threshold logic.

entry_Challenge.name: Damage / hit flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that changes when the player takes damage.

entry_Challenge.name: Invincibility / i-frames
entry_Challenge.tNote: A short-lived value active during post-hit recovery.

entry_Challenge.name: Boss HP (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Only valid while a boss encounter is active.

entry_Challenge.name: Boss HP (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Useful for phase-threshold logic.

entry_Challenge.name: Boss phase index
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which phase of a multi-phase boss encounter is currently active.

entry_Challenge.name: Player X position
entry_Challenge.tNote: Horizontal coordinate. Useful for hidden room entry and positional achievements.

entry_Challenge.name: Player Y position
entry_Challenge.tNote: Vertical coordinate. May be adjacent to X or at a separate address.

entry_Challenge.name: Enemy kill counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count of enemies defeated. May be global, per-type, or per-area.

entry_Challenge.name: Death counter (session)
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many times the player has died in the current run.

entry_Challenge.name: Death counter (total)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Lifetime death count. May be stored in save data rather than active RAM.

entry_Challenge.name: Play timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: Session or total play time. May be a float, frame counter, integer in hex, BCD, centiseconds in a single address, or split across hours, minutes, seconds, and centiseconds.

entry_Challenge.name: Combo counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: Consecutive hits or kills without taking damage.

entry_Challenge.name: Special resource (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Mana, stamina, or the game's equivalent. Deplete it to isolate.

entry_Challenge.name: Special resource (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Maximum capacity. May change with upgrades.

entry_Challenge.name: Score / points
entry_Challenge.tNote: If the game has a score system.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 1
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many times character 1 has cleared the game.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 2
entry_Challenge.tNote: Same for character 2.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 3
entry_Challenge.tNote: Continue for each playable character.
